what_problem_it_solves: Lets the owner inspect the exact earlier passage the current note points back to.
main_benefit: Fast recovery of lost author-flow context without leaving the unit.
main_risk: Can still be too thin if it points only to labels and not enough actual source text.
preferred_use_case: Specific reference-back cases where the earlier passage is conceptually needed.

what_problem_it_solves: Shows the relevant earlier excerpts that are tightly tied to the current one.
main_benefit: Preserves excerpt-based navigation where the dependency maps cleanly to a small excerpt cluster.
main_risk: Can become chaotic when one reference spans a larger passage split into many excerpts.
preferred_use_case: When the earlier discussion already exists as a compact coherent excerpt set.

what_problem_it_solves: Restores the larger original passage surrounding or preceding the excerpt.
main_benefit: Recovers a large portion of lost context with maximum fidelity to the source.
main_risk: Can reduce focus if shown too aggressively instead of as an intentional support layer.
preferred_use_case: When a reference-back actually targets a broader passage or chapter slice.

what_problem_it_solves: Adds a concise explanation of what is missing and why the owner may want to inspect earlier material.
main_benefit: Can orient the owner quickly before they open a larger support layer.
main_risk: A note alone may become a false substitute for the missing source material.
preferred_use_case: Secondary aid layered around, not replacing, source-preserving context restoration.

what_problem_it_solves: Preserves canonical unexcerpted chapter-level reference material when that is the safest support surface.
main_benefit: Avoids forcing the owner through long chains of excerpt-to-excerpt hops.
main_risk: Must be clearly framed as a reference layer so it does not blur the excerpt-centered workflow.
preferred_use_case: Cases where the author’s reference-back is really chapter- or passage-level, not excerpt-level.
